scope_note: QA Fibonacci Frobenius Order Identity cert [423]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p); F_n=0 mod p criterion); Lagrange, J.-L. (1771) 'Demonstration d'un theoreme d'arithmetique' Nouveaux Memoires de l'Academie de Berlin pp. 125-173 (order of element divides group size; ord(g) | p-1 for g in (Z/pZ)×); Hecke, E. (1920) 'Eine neue Art von Zetafunktionen...' MZ 6 pp. 11-51 (GL_1 Frobenius equidistribution over Q(sqrt 5)). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For split primes p ((5/p)=+1), the rank of apparition alpha(p) equals the multiplicative order of the Frobenius eigenvalue ratio rho = phi_tilde/psi_tilde in (Z/pZ)×, where phi_tilde = (1+s5)*inv(2,p) mod p (canonical golden ratio, cert [421]) and psi_tilde = 1-phi_tilde. ALGEBRAIC IDENTITIES: (ID1) phi_tilde*psi_tilde = -1 mod p (product of roots of x^2-x-1 = -constant = -(-1)/1 = -1; equivalently psi_tilde = -phi_tilde^{-1}); (ID2) phi_tilde/psi_tilde = -phi_tilde^2 mod p (since psi_tilde^{-1} = -phi_tilde, so phi_tilde/psi_tilde = phi_tilde*(-phi_tilde) = -phi_tilde^2 = -(phi_tilde+1) using phi_tilde^2=phi_tilde+1). FROBENIUS ORDER IDENTITY PROOF: F_n=0 mod p iff (by Binet mod p, cert [421]) phi_tilde^n = psi_tilde^n in (Z/pZ) iff (phi_tilde/psi_tilde)^n = 1 iff rho^n = 1 in (Z/pZ)×. Therefore the smallest n>=1 with F_n=0 mod p (i.e. alpha(p)) equals the smallest n>=1 with rho^n=1 (i.e. ord_{(Z/pZ)×}(rho)). This is the GL_1/Q(sqrt 5) Langlands statement: the Hecke eigenvalue at P (a prime above p in Z[phi]) is phi_tilde; the Frobenius at P is the identity (since p splits); the relative Frobenius ratio rho = phi_tilde/psi_tilde encodes the difference between the two eigenvalues phi_tilde and psi_tilde; its order in GL_1(F_p) = (Z/pZ)× is exactly alpha(p). ARTIN CONSTANT PREDICTION (C4): among split primes, those with alpha(p)=p-1 (i.e. rho is a primitive root mod p) have density approaching Artin's constant A = product_{q prime} (1 - 1/(q(q-1))) ~ 0.3739... within split primes. Empirical: 216/609 split primes <= 10000 are primitive (fraction=0.3547), consistent with Artin A=0.3739 (Artin's conjecture unconditional for specific elements remains open; GRH would give the exact asymptotic). CHECKS: (C1) PRODUCT IDENTITY: phi_tilde*psi_tilde=-1 mod p for all 45 split primes <=500 (algebraic identity from Vieta; falsifiable: any p where product != -1 refutes); (C2) RATIO IDENTITY: phi_tilde/psi_tilde=-phi_tilde^2 mod p for all 45 split primes <=500; (C3) FROBENIUS ORDER IDENTITY: ord_{(Z/pZ)×}(phi_tilde/psi_tilde) = alpha(p) for all 45/45 split primes <=500; falsifiable: any split prime p where the order disagrees with alpha(p) refutes; (C4) PRIMITIVE PRIMES: fraction of split primes <=10000 with alpha(p)=p-1 is 0.3547 in (0.25,0.50); consistent with Artin A~0.3739. LANGLANDS CHAIN: [421] delta(p) is phi-slope in Z/p^2 Z -> [422] delta(p)/p equidistributed (Hecke) -> [423] alpha(p) = ord_{GL_1(F_p)}(phi/psi) for split p (Frobenius order identity, split case). Pair with [424] (inert Frobenius) to complete the GL_1 Frobenius order story for all non-5 primes. All integer arithmetic; no float in QA layer. Derived 2026-06-14.
